rows.title: ✦ BUILD-A-PATCHWORK GOD ✦
rows.titleText: Hello, I'm Entity 43! And today I have quite the offer for you. I will be granting you powers to do things that creatures in your universe can only dream about. You may be wondering what the price for such a thing is, and the answer to that is.. well, your old form. You see, your body is a temple, and we're going to remodel it together.

objects.title: Transient Doppelganger Soul
objects.text: EASY MODE. Your soul is no longer a single fixed shape — it has become a shifting mirror, a transient doppelganger of every form you have ever claimed. With this, every section below loses its pick limit. Take as many heads, arms, eyes, hearts, legs, bodies, organs, and deaths as you desire. You phase between them like switching a build in a video game: only the parts you currently manifest occupy physical reality, the rest slumber in a private soul-space within you. Switching is fast — roughly a second per swap, and you can swap mid-combat with practice.

objects.title: Meta
objects.text: Unlocks the META choices hidden in every section below. Meta options draw power from OTHER fictional settings — anime, manga, comics, webnovels, wargames, and so on. Each one is clearly marked with a [META] prefix in its title and is hidden until you take this. These options are roughly equal in power to the strongest canon options (Zeus's Head, Arm of Hermes) — that is to say, city-affecting at minimum, world-altering at peak.

rows.title: Soul Mode
rows.titleText:
Before we begin carving you up, let's talk about your SOUL. Two special modes exist for the discerning supplicant:

• TRANSIENT DOPPELGANGER SOUL — Easy Mode. Your soul becomes fluid. You can phase limbs, organs, even heads 'in and out' like switching loadouts in a video game. This lets you pick MULTIPLE options from every section below (no longer limited to 1 head, 2 arms, 2 eyes — collect as many as you wish and phase between them at will). Phased-out parts slumber in a private soul-space.

• META — Unlocks the Meta sub-choices scattered throughout every section below. These options are inspired by other fictional settings (anime, manga, comics, webnovels, wargames). They are clearly marked with a [META] prefix in their titles. Without this toggle, you cannot see or select them.

Take one, take both, or take neither. Your humanity is the price either way.

objects.title: Elf
objects.text:
I will add a few hundred years onto your life span, make you slightly taller, and make your ears pointy. Later we will see if I feel like using scissors or my godly powers to do so. As the most boring 'fantasy' creature ever, you also become subtly prejudiced against non-elves — a low-grade superiority complex you can suppress with effort but never quite extinguish.
On the upside, you also gain: perfect archery instincts, supernatural grace (you cannot be clumsy even when drunk), low-light vision, an instinctive bond with forests (trees will lean out of your way), and a passive magical affinity that makes learning any form of magic 3× faster. Your lifespan is now measured in millennia, not centuries.

objects.title: Dwarf
objects.text:
An easy change for me. I'll cut your height in half, make you wider, and if you want I will make you more muscular. Slightly less boring than an elf.
Perks: You can see in absolute darkness, your stamina is effectively bottomless for physical labor, you have an instinctive mastery of any tool or weapon you pick up (not skill, just zero learning curve), and poisons that would kill a horse barely make you drowsy. You can also drink anyone under the table. You can sense the presence of precious metals and quality stone within fifty meters.

addons.text: I'm going to toss a fragment of your combat-rage at a random ork in Warhammer. They'll appreciate it. This makes your own bloodlust build slightly slower in combat (+1 minute of clarity before the rage kicks in).

objects.title: Orc
objects.text: I'll make you buff, green, and maybe tusked. Pick any orc from any universe — Warhammer, Azarath, Ennor, Warcraft, Elder Scrolls, your call. Either way, you become immense: at minimum twice a normal human's strength, dense bones that shrug off small-arms fire, and a healing factor that closes non-lethal wounds in minutes.

objects.title: Werewolf
objects.text: Whenever the moon rises you gain the ability to shift into a werewolf, adding a few feet to your height and making you strong enough to throw around trucks. Your claws can shred car doors, your bite transmits a weak version of the curse (victims become wolf-shifters bound to the same moon), and you can track any scent across a city.

objects.title: Kobold
objects.text:
Let there be kobolds! I'll make you a small reptilian humanoid, increasing your endurance and making you a bit more clumsy. I'll grant you a breath ability based on whatever color you pick: bright blue exhales lightning strong enough to stun a horse, dark blue exhales water at pressures that can cut steel, red exhales fire hot enough to melt aluminum, green exhales poison gas, white exhales freezing mist, black exhales acid. Pick a hybrid color for a hybrid breath.
You also gain: an instinctive talent for trap-making (your traps always work slightly better than they should), perfect darkvision, the ability to eat almost anything organic for sustenance, and a swarming coordination aura — any kobolds within a mile of you become smarter, braver, and follow your lead.
Wait. did you mean the furry version? If so, you won't get a breath weapon but you'll gain an impossibly keen sense of smell (comparable to a bloodhound on steroids) and a supernatural charm that makes canines obey you.

objects.title: Gnome
objects.text:
Similar to being a dwarf, but you will be even smaller, not have the muscles, and be about the same proportions as a human. You'll become immortal for as long as you're entertained by the world — if you spend a week bored, you'll quickly age into your seventies.
Perks: you have a supernatural knack for illusion and minor reality-bending (small objects may simply be where you swear they should be), animals are instinctively tame around you, and you can speak with any small woodland creature. You're also preternaturally lucky — small misfortunes consistently miss you.

objects.title: Beastmen
objects.text:
You can select any creature that currently exists in your reality to become a hybrid with — hell, I'll even throw in the extinct ones. For example, if you picked a bull you would gain increased strength and speed, grow two horns and a tail, gain a liking for grass, and become more aggressive. Pick a tiger for claws and apex reflexes, a spider for wall-climbing and web-spinning, an eagle for flight and telescopic sight, a shark for underwater breathing and an electrosense, an elephant for immense strength and a prehensile trunk, a chameleon for active camouflage, anything.
Whatever you pick, the hybrid form is vastly stronger than the base creature. You also retain the ability to shift between full-human, hybrid, and full-animal forms at will.

objects.title: Celestial Heritage
objects.text:
Ah, want a touch of divinity, aye? I can do that, pick whatever you want.
Angel grants you wings that allow true flight at Mach 2, a passive halo of protection that turns aside small arms, and good-natured people instinctively trust you (even those with bad karma, as long as they TRY to be good).
Demon blood makes you 5× stronger, grants you a tail in whatever shape you wish, and those of an evil nature go out of their way to befriend you. You can also summon hellfire that burns without fuel.
Fae gives you a green hue, drastically improves all things creative, and those who value entertainment above all else take a liking to you. You can also make binding magical oaths.
Yokai (Japanese spirit) gives you an animal form, supernatural longevity, and access to one minor Yokai power of your choice (illusions, shapeshifting, fire-manipulation, etc.).
These are just a few of the celestials you can choose from.

objects.title: Dragonborn
objects.text:
Draconic heritage made flesh. You grow scales hard enough to stop rifle rounds, gain a powerful breath weapon matching your chosen dragon color (fire, frost, lightning, acid, or poison), and develop an instinctive command of any dragon you encounter — they will treat you as kin, not prey.
At full power, you can briefly manifest wings and a tail, your breath weapon can level a small building in one shot, and your scales shimmer with elemental energy that harms anyone who strikes you in melee. you can enter 'Dragon Aspect': your size doubles, your breath becomes a torrent, and you gain limited flight.

objects.title: Naga / Lamia
objects.text:
Your lower body becomes a powerful serpent tail, twenty feet long and strong enough to crush a car. You gain a hypnotic gaze that can compel weak-willed creatures (and distract strong ones), a venomous bite whose toxin you can swap between paralytic, hallucinogenic, and necrotic at will, and the ability to shed your skin once a month to fully heal any non-lethal wound.
You can swim at sixty miles per hour, slither at forty on land, and sense heat through walls within thirty meters. Your scales are as tough as Kevlar.

objects.title: Arachne
objects.text:
Your lower body becomes that of a giant spider — eight legs, an abdomen the size of a small car. You can produce silk stronger than steel cable at a rate of a mile per hour, climb any surface (including glass), and sense vibrations through your webs across an entire city block.
You can build traps in seconds that can hold a charging rhino. Your venom liquefies internal organs; you can drink the result. You have eight eyes (two human, six spider) giving you 360° awareness.

objects.title: Harpy
objects.text:
Your arms become wings (with hands at the mid-joint, like a bat), your legs become taloned bird-legs, and your chest barrels out to anchor flight muscles. You can fly at 90 mph for hours, dive at 200 mph, and your talons can punch through car roofs.
You can summon localized wind gusts strong enough to flip cars, mimic any bird call perfectly, and your screech can shatter glass within fifty meters. Your feathers are as tough as steel plating.

objects.title: Merfolk
objects.text:
Your lower body becomes a powerful fish tail. Underwater, you can swim at 80 mph, breathe freely, see clearly in any water condition, and command sea-life within a mile. You can speak to the ocean itself, and it sometimes listens — small miracles of current and tide come to your aid.
On land, your tail can shift into legs (a slow, painful transformation), but you must keep your skin wet or you'll desiccate within hours. You can manipulate water within line of sight, moving up to 10,000 gallons at a time.

objects.title: Centaur
objects.text:
Your lower body becomes that of a powerful horse. You can gallop at 70 mph for hours, leap 30 feet from a standstill, and your hooves can shatter engine blocks. Your human upper body retains full dexterity, allowing you to fire a bow from horseback with perfect accuracy at full gallop.
You have the stamina of a horse (immense), the digestion of a horse (you can eat grass if you must), and a supernatural bond with all equines.

objects.title: Tengu
objects.text:
Japanese bird-spirit. You grow a pair of wings (color of your choice), your nose elongates into a beak-like shape, and your hair becomes feathered. You can fly at 100 mph, summon localized storms, and possess an instinctive mastery of any blade you hold.
You can speak with any bird, see the future in broad strokes (a single cryptic image, never false), and your swordplay can cut through dimensions —, a single sword strike can open a rift to anywhere you've been before.

objects.title: Kitsune
objects.text:
Fox-spirit heritage. You grow fox ears and a single tail (more tails manifest as you grow in power, up to nine over a century). You gain supernatural grace, the ability to create perfect illusions that fool all five senses of anyone caught in them, and a fox-form you can shift into instantly.
You can produce foxfire — floating orbs of pale flame that you can shape, throw, or use to light areas. With practice, your illusions can become semi-real (illusionary fire still burns). You age incredibly slowly; a century passes like a decade.

objects.title: Jötun
objects.text: Norse giant-kin. You grow to twelve feet tall, your skin becomes cold to the touch and slightly blue-tinged, and your strength is enough to flip a city bus one-handed. You are immune to cold (down to absolute zero) and can manipulate ice: freeze a swimming pool solid in seconds, create ice constructs, walk on water by freezing it beneath your feet. you can manifest a 'Jötun form': 30 feet tall, strength enough to demolish a small building with bare hands, and the ability to call a localized blizzard.

objects.title: Svartálf
objects.text:
Norse dark elf. Your skin darkens to deep grey or blue-black, your eyes become solid black, and you gain the ability to merge with and travel through shadows. You can step into one shadow and emerge from any other shadow you can see, up to a mile away.
You can fashion shadow into solid objects (weapons, shields, simple tools) that last as long as you concentrate. You're invisible in absolute darkness. You also gain supernatural skill at crafting — anything you make is of master quality.

objects.title: Ifrit Heritage
objects.text:
Fire genie heritage. Your skin takes on a reddish-bronze hue, your hair flickers like flame when emotional, and you can produce and control fire at will — enough to engulf a city block in inferno. You're completely immune to fire and heat, can absorb existing flames to heal yourself, and can grant a single wish (the wish is always granted, but with a creative monkey's-paw twist that favors you, not the wisher — unless the wisher is you, in which case it's straight).
You can take on a partial genie form (smoke from the waist down) for limited flight and intangibility.

objects.title: Ghoul
objects.text:
Undead cannibal heritage. You can eat any decayed organic matter without harm (in fact, it heals you), your jaws can bite through bone, and your claws can shred cinderblock. You can sense the recently dead within a mile and consume their memories by eating their flesh.
You regenerate from any non-lethal wound in hours; even severed limbs regrow over weeks. You're immune to disease and poison. you can enter 'kagune form' — manifested organic weapons that can extend up to 30 feet, strong enough to impale a tank.

objects.title: Valkyrie Heritage
objects.text: Norse chooser of the slain. You grow wings (white, grey, or black — your choice), can fly at 150 mph, and can see the 'shine of death' on anyone within your sight — those about to die glow faintly, those destined for great violence glow brighter. You can also see the shine on yourself, warning you of approaching danger. you can claim a soul at the moment of death (you must be present) and either guide it to an afterlife of your choice or bind it as an spectral companion that fights alongside you. You can have up to seven bound souls at once.

objects.title: Oni
objects.text:
Japanese ogre-demon. You grow to eight feet tall, your skin turns red or blue (your choice), you grow one or two horns, and your strength doubles. You can manifest a kanabō (iron studded club) made of spiritual energy that can shatter tank armor.
You're immune to physical pain (you still take damage, you just don't feel it), can drink any amount of alcohol without ill effect, and gain power from anger — the angrier you get, the stronger and tougher you become, with no upper limit during a fight (resets after combat ends)

objects.title: Aasimar
objects.text: Celestial-touched human. Your heritage includes a touch of the Upper Planes — your eyes glow faintly, your features are idealized, and your presence is calming. You gain: innate goodness (you radiate an aura of trust; the innocent and good-natured instinctively trust you), minor celestial powers, and the ability to detect evil by sight. You're also naturally long-lived (5× normal human lifespan).

objects.title: Tiefling
objects.text: Infernal-touched human. Your heritage includes a touch of the Lower Planes — small horns, a tail, glowing eyes. You gain: innate cunning (you're 2× smarter than baseline human, with a talent for deception and persuasion), minor infernal powers, and resistance to fire and poison. You're also naturally long-lived (5× normal human lifespan).

objects.title: Genasi
objects.text: Elemental-touched human. Your heritage includes a touch of one of the elemental planes (your choice: Air, Earth, Fire, or Water). You gain: control over your chosen element (summon, shape, command — strong enough to demolish a building with sustained effort), immunity to your element's harmful effects, and the ability to transform partially into your element (become living fire, a cloud of air, a stone golem, or a wave of water).

objects.title: [META] Saiyan Heritage (Dragon Ball)
objects.text: Your body carries the Saiyan genome from the Dragon Ball universe. You gain: the ability to transform into different Super Saiyan forms (each form multiplies your power 50× — Super Saiyan, Super Saiyan 2, Super Saiyan 3, with mastery). You start at Super Saiyan tier (5× human strength, speed, and durability), and can transform further under emotional stress or with training. You also gain: the ability to fire ki blasts (energy projectiles strong enough to demolish buildings), fly at supersonic speeds, and sense the power levels of others. After near-death injuries, you receive a massive permanent power boost (the Zenkai effect).

objects.title: [META] Quincy Blood (Bleach)
objects.text: Your blood carries Quincy spiritual energy. You gain: the ability to manifest spirit-weapons (bows, swords, etc. made of pure spiritual energy — strong enough to cut through dimensions), the power to absorb ambient spiritual energy to fuel your attacks, and the ability to see and interact with spiritual entities (ghosts, hollows, soul reapers). You can also enter 'Letzt Stil' (final form) — a 10× power boost, but you must train to maintain it safely.

objects.title: [META] Esper (Toaruverse)
objects.text: Your brain develops as an Esper — a personal reality user from the Toaru universe. You gain: a single defined personal reality (a personal physics-defying power of your design — electromaster, telekinesist, vector manipulator, etc.). The power is calculated at level 4 to start (city-affecting potential), and grows with use toward level 5 (continental). You also gain the ability to perceive and compute physical phenomena at superhuman speed, letting you predict and counter attacks.

objects.title: [META] Kryptonian Heritage (DC)
objects.text: Your body carries Kryptonian genetics from the DC universe. Under any yellow sun (like Earth's), you gain: superhuman strength (capable of moving planets at peak), superhuman speed (faster than light), flight, invulnerability, heat vision, x-ray vision, telescopic/microscopic vision, super-hearing, and super-breath. Under a red sun, you lose all powers. Kryptonite is your weakness — green kryptonite weakens and can kill you, other colors have varied effects.

objects.title: [META] Vulcan Heritage (Star Trek)
objects.text: Your body carries Vulcan genetics from Star Trek. You gain: a Vulcan's superior strength (3× human), enhanced hearing and vision, the ability to perform a mind meld (touch-based telepathy — read minds, share thoughts, transfer consciousness), the Vulcan nerve pinch (render any humanoid unconscious with a precise neck strike), and the Kolinahr disciplines (perfect emotional control through logic). You also gain a katric arc — the ability to transfer your consciousness (your katra) to another host at the moment of death.

objects.title: [META] Viltrumite Heritage (Invincible)
objects.text: Your body carries Viltrumite genetics from the Invincible comic universe. You gain: superhuman strength (capable of lifting hundreds of tons at peak), superhuman speed, flight, extreme durability (immune to conventional weapons, can survive in vacuum), and an extraordinarily long lifespan (thousands of years with youthful vitality). Your power grows continuously as you age and train — a Viltrumite in their prime can devastate planets. You also gain the Viltrumite will — an unbreakable determination that lets you fight through injuries that would kill any normal being.

objects.title: [META] Royal Viltrumite Heritage (Invincible)
objects.text: You carry the genetics of Royal Viltrumite blood — the elite of the Viltrumite Empire. All Viltrumite powers are amplified 10× beyond the standard: strength capable of shattering continents, speed approaching light, durability that tanks nuclear weapons, and a lifespan measured in millions of years. Royal Viltrumites also gain: the Smart Atom manipulation (alter atomic structure of matter on touch), the ability to sense other Viltrumites across star systems, and the Royal Command — a voice of authority that weaker beings instinctively obey.

objects.title: [META] Claymore Hybrid (Claymore)
objects.text: Your body becomes a Claymore — a hybrid of human and Yoma (demon) flesh from the Claymore manga. You gain: superhuman strength, speed, and reflexes (10× human), the ability to sense Yoki (demonic energy) within a mile, rapid regeneration (minor wounds close in seconds), and a signature weapon — a massive claymore sword that you can wield with one hand. You can release up to 80% of your Yoki for massive power boosts (50× stats) without losing control.

objects.title: [META] Goblin Evolution (Reincarnated as a Goblin)
objects.text: Your body carries the evolution system from the webnovel 'Reincarnated as a Goblin'. You start as a weak goblin but can consume defeated foes to gain their traits and evolve into stronger forms — Hobgoblin, Ogre, Ogre Lord, Demon, eventually a Demon Lord. Each evolution multiplies your power. You also gain the 'Great Sage' — an internal advisor that analyzes situations, suggests optimal actions, and answers questions. You can also bestow names on subordinates, evolving them as well.

objects.title: [META] Slime Body (Tensei Slime)
objects.text: Your body becomes a slime — specifically, a Rimuru Tempest-style slime from 'That Time I Got Reincarnated as a Slime'. You gain: the ability to absorb and analyze anything you consume (skills, magic, items, even attacks), shapeshifting (take any form you've absorbed), infinite stamina, immunity to physical attacks (your slime body just absorbs them), and the Great Sage + Predator skills. As you absorb more, you evolve into stronger forms (Demon Slime, True Demon Lord, eventually Ultimate Skill wielder).

objects.title: [META] Saiyan Royal Blood (Dragon Ball)
objects.text: You carry the royal bloodline of the Saiyan race — Vegeta's lineage. All Saiyan benefits (Super Saiyan transformations, ki blasts, Zenkai boost) are amplified. You start at Super Saiyan 2 tier, can transform faster, and have access to the royal techniques: the Galick Gun (signature purple energy beam), the Big Bang Attack (devastating sphere of energy), and the Final Flash (planet-destroying beam). Your potential ceiling is higher than a standard Saiyan.

objects.title: [META] Eternal (Marvel Comics)
objects.text: Your body carries Eternal genetics from Marvel Comics. You gain: cosmic energy manipulation (project energy blasts, fly at FTL speeds, survive in any environment), immortality (you don't age, regenerate from any wound), superhuman physical abilities (strength to lift 100 tons, reflexes 100× human), and the ability to focus your cosmic energy into a single signature power of your choice (energy projection, matter manipulation, telepathy, telekinesis, etc.). You're essentially a minor god.

objects.title: [META] Inhuman Heritage (Marvel Comics)
objects.text: Your body carries Inhuman genetics from Marvel Comics. You gain: a unique superpower of your design (activated by Terrigen Mist exposure at character creation), enhanced physical abilities (5× human baseline), and a longer lifespan (5× normal). Your power can be anything within reason: elemental control, shapeshifting, teleportation, energy projection, etc. The power is fixed but grows in strength with use.

objects.title: [META] Ghoul (Tokyo Ghoul)
objects.text: Your body becomes a Ghoul from Tokyo Ghoul. You gain: a kagune (a predatory organ that manifests as tentacle-like weapons from your back — strong enough to cut steel, extendable up to 30 feet, regenerates if severed), superhuman physical abilities (5× human), a healing factor (regenerate from any non-lethal wound in minutes), and a ghoul's senses (smell blood from a mile, see in darkness). Your kagune type (ukaku, koukaku, rinkaku, bikaku) is your choice, each with different properties.

objects.title: [META] Wizard (Harry Potter)
objects.text: Your body carries wizard genetics from the Harry Potter universe. You gain: the ability to perform magic with a wand (combat spells like Stupefy, shielding spells, elemental magic, transfiguration, charms, hexes, curses), the ability to perform wandless magic (weaker but always available), magical perception (see Dementors, Boggarts, magical creatures invisible to Muggles), and a magical core that grows with practice. You can learn any spell from the Harry Potter universe with study.

objects.title: [META] Demigod (Percy Jackson)
objects.text: Your body carries demigod genetics from the Percy Jackson universe. You gain: superhuman physical abilities (5× human), ADHD (actually battle reflexes — you instinctively react to threats faster than normal humans), dyslexia (actually your brain is hardwired for Ancient Greek), and the blessing of a Greek or Roman god of your choice — granting you a single defined power based on that god's domain (Poseidon's water control, Zeus's lightning, Athena's strategic genius, Ares's combat mastery, etc.).

objects.title: [META] Magus (Fate/Stay Night)
objects.text: Your body carries a Magic Circuit system from the Fate universe. You gain: the ability to generate and channel Od (personal magical energy), 30 Magic Circuits of high quality, the capacity to learn and cast any Nasuverse magecraft (runes, Jewel Magecraft, Formalcraft, Bounded Fields, Reinforcement, Gradation Air), and a single specialized magecraft of your design that you have natural talent for. Your circuits regenerate Od over time.

rows.title: Species
rows.titleText:
First up on the list of sacrificing your humanity: your humanity.

You can take as many options here as you to, I'll sort out the details of how that works later, or I guess you could just stay same ol'boring human.

objects.title: True Immortality
objects.text:
Nothing can kill ya. That's it. Not the sun, not a gun, not a bun you chocked upon, and to break the rhyming, not even the end of the universe itself — though if that does happen I'm making my own universe as a side project, you're invited to stay in it after yours dies if you want. People say eventually life would get boring — they're wrong. Spend ten years in a dark cave and the sky will be the most entertaining thing ever when you emerge.
As you may have pieced together, you're sacrificing stuff, not just getting freebies. So: your memory becomes perfect — once you experience it all, you remember it all forever. If you visit my universe, I'll make your memories a bit fuzzy so you're less boring. And yes, the existing.

objects.title: Body Snatcher
objects.text:
Whenever you die your soul will search for a new host — normally one with a desired aesthetic and young. Your soul will not instantly take over; you'll become a passenger within them until they agree to give their body to you. It would be inconvenient for them to die beforehand, wouldn't it? So you'll be able to grant them usage of any of your powers whenever you wish.
You can 'hop' between hosts at will once you've been invited in — leave one body for another you've previously occupied, or possess an unborn child (with the parents' souls' implicit consent). Your original powers come with you between bodies.

objects.title: Vampire
objects.text:
You get to live forever have strength five times that of a normal human, never need to sleep, and your reflexes triple. Why would anyone not want to be one? Well, besides them being the elves of the horror world, the downsides: never seeing the sun again (unless you wish to die — sun exposure reduces you to ash within a minute), and needing to slurp the blood of your fellow man at a frequency that won't allow you to play detective to ensure you only feed on 'evil' people.
On the upside: you can sire other vampires (who are bound to you as fledglings), you can transform into a wolf, a flock of bats, or mist at will, and you can climb walls like a spider. Also, I'll fix that 300-year-storage limit humans have and give you an enhanced memory.

objects.title: Undead
objects.text:
I'll make it so rot and age will not kill you, but it's up to you to keep your body all nice and pretty. As for every other option, I will improve your memory — not to perfection, though.
Perks of being Undead: you can animate your own detached body parts (a severed hand can crawl around and do your bidding), you feel no pain, you do not need to breathe or eat (though you can if you wish), and you can enter a deathlike torpor to fake your own death convincingly. You can also see and speak with ghosts.

objects.title: Skost
objects.text:
You'll become a skull ghost, which is your head with a generic cartoon ghost body beneath it. People can see and hear you, but you can't pick up stuff unless someone alive hands it to you. Warning: people can still punch you (you have ghostly physicality when you will it).
On the upside: you can fly, become invisible at will, walk through walls, possess inanimate objects (your skull ghost can pilot a car, a robot, a statue), and freeze rooms with your presence. You can also eat memories of the living — one memory per touch, yours to keep. The more memories you eat, the more solid your ghost-body becomes until you can interact with the physical world freely.

objects.title: Phoenix Rebirth
objects.text:
Upon death, your body immolates in a conflagration that scorches a 50-meter radius, and from the ashes you are reborn — at the age of 25, in perfect health, with all memories intact. Each rebirth makes you slightly stronger (+5% to all physical attributes, permanent).
You can also trigger a voluntary immolation: a burst of regenerating flame that heals any wound, cures any disease, and purges any toxin, but leaves you exhausted. You can produce and control fire at will.

objects.title: Reincarnation Cycle (Death)
objects.text:
Buddhist-style. Upon death, you reincarnate as a newborn child somewhere in the world, retaining all memories and personality. The reincarnation is karmic: if you've lived virtuously, you'll be born into a good life; if not, well. your next body's circumstances will reflect your past deeds. You retain all powers across lives.
You can also access a limited 'akashic memory' — fragments of your past lives that surface as intuition and skills. With meditation, you can deliberately recall past-life knowledge.

objects.title: Phylactery
objects.text:
Classic lich. Your soul is bound to an object of your choosing (a ring, a book, a gem, anything you own). As long as the phylactery exists, you cannot truly die — destroyed bodies reform within 24 hours near the phylactery, restored to full health.
While you have a phylactery, your magical power doubles (if you have any magic), and you can sense its location at all times. You can have multiple phylacteries (one per death-option sacrifice), but each additional one splits your soul further — your mind becomes more fractured and eccentric.

objects.title: Eternal Champion
objects.text:
Moorcockian. Upon death, you are reborn as a new champion somewhere in the multiverse — same soul, different body, different name, different life, but always a warrior or hero of some kind. You retain your powers and gain a new 'aspect' (a minor new power themed to your new life) each incarnation.
You can be summoned across incarnations by those who know the right ritual (you decide if you answer). You also have a supernatural destiny — you are always at the center of great conflicts.

objects.title: Bardo Walker
objects.text:
Tibetan Book of the Dead style. Upon death, you enter the Bardo — a liminal dream-world where you can choose your next form: a new body in this world, an animal, a spirit, an entity in another reality, or you can choose to dissolve into pure awareness for a time before re-emerging.
While in the Bardo (which lasts 49 days), you can interact with other dying souls, encounter spirits, and glimpse possible futures. Your choices in the Bardo shape your next incarnation. You retain all powers and memories across forms.

objects.title: Time Loop
objects.text: Groundhog Day, but limited. Upon death, you reset to a 'save point' you set (must be a moment in the last 24 hours). You retain all memories; everything else resets. You cannot reset to before the moment you received this power. you can voluntarily reset without dying. Each reset, your body's muscle memory and reflexes from the previous loop are partially retained (+2% to physical skills per loop, capping at +50% cumulative)

objects.title: Ghost Walk
objects.text:
You can leave your body at will as a ghost, traveling the world as an invisible, intangible spirit. Your body remains in a coma while you're gone; if it's killed while you're out, you become a permanent ghost (with all the skull-ghost perks and limitations from the Skost option).
As a ghost you can: fly, become invisible, walk through walls, possess sleeping people (they wake with no memory of what you did), and consume ambient emotions to sustain yourself. You can return to your body at any time, regardless of distance.

objects.title: Hourai Immortality
objects.text:
Touhou-style absolute immortality. You cannot die. Period. Any wound, no matter how lethal, regenerates within seconds to minutes (a vaporized body reforms over an hour). You do not age. You will outlast the universe itself.
You can also resurrect at will any being whose flesh you've consumed within the past year — they return to life at your side, bound to you as a friend (not a slave)

objects.title: Buddhist Arhat
objects.text:
Upon death, you achieve Nirvana and become an Arhat — a being beyond the cycle of rebirth. You exist as a state of pure awareness, free of suffering, free of desire. You can manifest a physical body at will (any form you choose), travel anywhere instantly, and perceive all of reality directly.
Your wisdom is profound; your patience, boundless. You can teach others the path to enlightenment, and those who follow your teachings may escape rebirth themselves.

objects.title: Lichdom (Skeletal)
objects.text:
Your flesh falls away, leaving an animate skeleton. You retain all your powers and gain undead immunity to poison, disease, fatigue, and pain. You can reassemble yourself from any amount of damage (even a single bone) over time. You do not need to eat, sleep, or breathe.
You can animate additional skeletal minions from any bones you touch. You can also swap bones with other skeletons to instantly learn skills they had in life.

objects.title: Reaper's Pact
objects.text: You become an agent of Death itself. You cannot die while there are still souls to reap — Death sustains you as long as you do your job. You can see the dying and the dead, harvest souls (sending them to their proper afterlife), and command lesser undead. you can 'reap' a willing or defeated soul, gaining a fragment of their power permanently. You also gain a scythe that can cut through anything physical, including dimensions (you can open short-lived rifts with a swing)

objects.title: Elysian Fields Pass
objects.text: Greek — a guaranteed ticket to the Elysian Fields, the paradise-realm for heroes. Upon death, your soul goes directly to Elysium — a land of eternal spring, peaceful rest, and reunion with other heroes. You can also visit Elysium in dreams while alive, consulting the heroes who reside there for advice. you can resurrect a hero from Elysium to aid you.

objects.title: Valhalla Pass
objects.text: Norse — a guaranteed seat in Valhalla. Upon death in battle (or any death you choose to frame as 'battle'), your soul goes to Valhalla — the hall of warriors, where you feast and fight eternally alongside Odin's einherjar. You can visit Valhalla in dreams while alive, train with the einherjar (gaining combat skills rapidly), and, summon a single einherjar warrior to fight alongside you.

objects.title: [META] Hōgyoku Immortality (Bleach)
objects.text:
The Hōgyoku, a wish-granting orb from the Bleach universe, implants itself in your chest. It grants your heart's deepest desire — and for most, that desire is power. The Hōgyoku makes you immortal: you cannot die by any means, you regenerate from any wound in seconds, and your power grows continuously the more you are pushed to your limits.
It will also grant one specific transformation based on your deepest desire (you define this at selection; the Hōgyoku manifests it). This transformation can be anything from a Bankai-style release to an Arrancar resurrection to a transcendent state.

objects.title: [META] Reiatsu Persistence (Bleach)
objects.text: Your soul is dense with reiatsu (spiritual pressure), making it nearly impossible to truly destroy. Upon death, your soul persists as a powerful spirit — you can interact with the physical world (move objects, manifest visibly), and slowly regenerate a new body over weeks. You retain all your powers in spirit form. The stronger your reiatsu, the harder it is to permanently kill you — at peak, even atomizing your body leaves your soul intact, ready to reform.

objects.title: [META] TARDIS-Linked Regeneration (Doctor Who)
objects.text: Your death-state becomes linked to Time Lord regeneration. When you suffer a fatal injury, your body regenerates — healing all wounds, but changing your physical appearance (and shifting aspects of your personality). You have 12 regenerations (13 lives total), each granting a slightly different physical form and personality while retaining all memories and powers. Each regeneration also boosts your resilience — you become harder to kill each time.

objects.title: [META] Immortality of the Undying (Undead Unluck)
objects.text: Your death-state becomes that of an Undying from Undead Unluck. You cannot die — ever. Any wound regenerates instantly (decapitation? head regrows in seconds). If your body is completely destroyed, you reform elsewhere within an hour. You also gain a 'Rule' — a supernatural law you can impose on reality by stating it (e.g., 'whoever touches me loses their luck', 'whoever I name becomes unlucky'). The Rule is fixed once established but is absolute while you live.

objects.title: [META] Ten Shadows Resurrection (JJK)
objects.text: Your death-state becomes linked to the Ten Shadows Technique from Jujutsu Kaisen. If you die, you can resurrect by sacrificing one of your shikigami (you start with 10 shadow shikigami you can summon). Each shikigami is a powerful supernatural beast. As long as you have shikigami remaining, you cannot truly die. You can also summon your shikigami to fight alongside you.

objects.title: [META] Dragon Ball Resurrection (Dragon Ball)
objects.text: Your death-state becomes that of a Dragon Ball character. When you die, your soul persists in the afterlife with all your powers intact — you can train, fight, and even visit the living world for short periods. You can be resurrected by any sufficiently powerful reality-warping effect (the Dragon Balls, a wish, etc.). While dead, you retain a halo visible to other supernatural beings.

objects.title: [META] Endbringer Core (Worm)
objects.text: Your death-state becomes that of an Endbringer from Worm. Your body is anchored to a 'core' — a dimensional anchor hidden elsewhere. As long as the core exists, your body regenerates from any damage, no matter how total (vaporized body reforms from the core in days). The core is small (size of a fist) and can be hidden anywhere in the multiverse. Only destroying the core can permanently kill you.

objects.title: [META] Conceptual Immortality (Type-Moon)
objects.text: Your death-state becomes conceptual — you cannot die as long as your concept exists in human consciousness. Choose your concept (a color, a number, an emotion, a natural phenomenon). As long as that concept exists in human thought, you regenerate from any wound, reform from complete destruction, and persist eternally. Only the complete erasure of your concept from all human minds can kill you.

objects.title: [META] Valhalla Einherjar (Record of Ragnarok)
objects.text: Your death-state becomes that of an Einherjar from Record of Ragnarok. Upon death, you go to Valhalla — but you can be temporarily resurrected for important battles. While resurrected, your power is amplified 100× (you become a champion of humanity). Each resurrection lasts for one battle or one day, after which you return to Valhalla. You can be resurrected an unlimited number of times.

rows.title: Death
rows.titleText:
Don't worry, we'll eventually get to sacrificing more important things, like your arms. But for now, we'll stick to the esoterics, which happens to be your death.

Pick as many as you like. They stack — each applies on the death that matches its trigger. If you have the Transient Doppelganger Soul, you can phase between death-states too (become a ghost for an hour, then resume your body, etc.).

objects.title: Arm of Hermes
objects.text:
This arm will allow you to command the very skies, allowing you to chop buildings in half with a swing and, after a bit of training, make whatever you wish to fly with the wind. The birds will love you, and insects will move out of your way. You can also fly yourself by directing wind beneath you, reaching speeds of 200 mph.
At full mastery, you can summon tornadoes (up to EF5), part clouds, redirect hurricanes, and create vacuum blades that cut through anything softer than diamond. With practice, you can suffocate an opponent by extracting the air from their lungs.

objects.title: Hephaestus' steady hand
objects.text:
All that you craft shall be of higher quality — what would be a steel sword becomes a masterwork steel sword that holds an edge for decades. What would be a clock becomes a clock that keeps perfect time for centuries. And if you craft something realistic enough — a sculpture, a painting, a sufficiently detailed doll — it comes to life, but it can only be as strong as the average Space Marine (still extremely dangerous).
You also gain a supernatural instinct for enchantment: any weapon, tool, or device you craft can hold one magical enchantment, no ritual required. You can disassemble and re-forge existing items to add enchantments to them.

objects.title: Roomate
objects.text:
During my travels in different universes I've found many a parasite. This is one of them. I'll lop your arm off, plant it on the new empty slot, and age it up. It will act like a normal arm for the most part, besides the fact that it has a stomach independent of yours and is sapient. Oh, right! The upsides: its roots will sink into you and consume any parasites or viruses that make their way into you, it will be protective of you, and it will likely grow to be your best friend (probably). It is also able to consume tanks as if they were paper.
The parasite-arm can extend up to 30 feet on tendrils, regrow if severed (over a week), and absorb biomass to grow stronger. After consuming a sufficiently powerful entity, it can briefly manifest that entity's physical traits.

objects.title: Ghost Hand
objects.text:
I stole it from your future dead self — don't worry, he doesn't need it anymore, after I consumed your gods their afterlives followed. With this you can summon ghosts to speak to and hang out with. They are just barely strong enough to use controllers. You can also command them to perform minor tasks (carry small objects, scout ahead, whisper in enemies' ears) — up to a dozen at once.
Your arm is a permanent ghost: it is intangible to the physical world but can interact freely with spirits, ghosts, and the undead. You can sense ghosts within a mile, and they can sense you (and tend to flock to you); you can temporarily make your arm physical (and a chosen ghost intangible).

objects.title: Arm Coded
objects.text:
Bet the right half of the rows not being round is starting to annoy ya. With this arm, that's now permanently code, you could just go ahead and fix that for yourself. It can also find passwords for whatever devices your empty socket touches, as well as any account passwords that have been typed on that device. It can also just code stuff for you, if you want it to.
Beyond passwords, your arm can: directly interface with any digital system via touch (you can read and rewrite code at the speed of thought), 'compile' physical objects from raw data if you have a sufficiently advanced fabricator, and project holographic interfaces into the air around you. you can 'edit' a physical law within a 10-meter radius (change gravity's direction, alter friction, swap cause and effect for a single action)

objects.title: Fist of the Inquisition
objects.text:
I have so many of these, one is sure to look just like your real arm! With this you can close rifts into the Fade and people will be quick to see you as a messiah figure. You can also smite demons on touch (calling down holy fire that burns evil beings, leaves the innocent unharmed), lay on hands to heal wounds and cure diseases, and sense supernatural evil within a hundred meters. you can manifest a 'Hand of Judgment' — a giant spectral fist that can punch through a building, smiting any evildoers inside. You can also create wards that bar supernatural evil from entering.
Downsides: demons now exist in the dream realm, but they do not yet have a way out. Also, remember how I said people would view you as a messiah? More than one group will attempt to claim you — have fun with the holy wars! Your every public action is now interpreted through a religious lens, whether you want it to be or not.

objects.title: Overlord's Gauntlet
objects.text:
The arm of a hero betrayed by their friends and brought back to be the big bad evil guy. With this gauntlet you will lead the warrior minion tribe. They are goblins. With this arm you can now collect life force from any living creature — the stronger the better — and use it to summon warrior minions from the spawning pit. Warrior minions are about four feet tall, brown, surprisingly strong for their size, fiercely loyal, and equipped with whatever weapons and armor you scavenge for them.
A spawning pit will slowly grow where you set up shop — a small hole in the ground that glows brown. You can have up to 50 warrior minions at once; killing the existing ones returns their life-force to the pit for re-summoning.

objects.title: Hand of Machina
objects.text:
The hand from the future god of Machines. With a touch, you can upgrade any object into a more advanced version of itself — a sword becomes sharper and self-repairing, a phone becomes a holographic communicator, a car becomes a self-driving electric vehicle, a rifle becomes a railgun. The improvement is significant but bounded: you can only perform three upgrades total a day.
Upgrades persist permanently. With enough time (weeks of daily upgrades on the same item), you can produce technology centuries ahead of the modern world.

objects.title: Arm of Tyr
objects.text: Norse god of war and sacrifice. Your right arm becomes infused with Tyr's essence: silver-skinned, supernaturally strong, and capable of generating a glowing rune-blade from the palm. You have perfect tactical awareness in any battle (you instinctively know the best move), and any warrior who fights alongside you gains +50% combat effectiveness. You can make a 'Tyr's Sacrifice' — willingly accept a permanent injury (lose a finger, an eye, a memory, a loved one) in exchange for a guaranteed victory in the next battle. The sacrifice is real and permanent; the victory is absolute (you cannot lose, fate itself bends to ensure it).

objects.title: Hand of Nuada
objects.text:
Celtic god-king of the Tuatha Dé Danann, he who lost his arm and had it replaced with one of silver. Your arm becomes pure silver — beautiful, perfect, supernaturally sharp at the fingertips. You gain absolute mastery of all bladed weapons; in your hand, any blade becomes an extension of your will.
You can also manifest a 'Sword of Light' (Claíomh Solais) — a glowing blade that never misses its target and severs anything it strikes (including enchantments, curses, and conceptual bonds)

objects.title: Vishnu's Many Arms
objects.text:
Hindu god of preservation. You gain four additional arms (six total), each able to wield a different divine weapon at once. You can summon the Sudarshana Chakra (a throwing disc that always returns, severs anything it strikes), the Kaumodaki Gada (a mace that shatters fortifications), the Sharanga bow (fires arrows of pure light that never miss), and the Nandaka sword (cuts through enchantments).
Each weapon can be used before needing to be re-summoned. With all six arms active, you can fight like a small army.

objects.title: Susanoo's Arm
objects.text: Japanese god of storms. Your arm crackles with perpetual lightning; you can call down lightning strikes at will (, each capable of destroying a vehicle), summon storms covering a square mile, and walk on wind currents for limited flight. you can manifest the Totsuka-no-Tsurugi — a sword of pure storm that seals anything it cuts into an eternal sake jar (a prison dimension). The sword can also cut through dimensional barriers.

objects.title: Hand of the Morrigan
objects.text:
Celtic phantom queen, goddess of war and fate. Your arm becomes spectral at will — able to phase through armor and flesh to grip an enemy's heart directly. You can curse warriors with 'the Morrigan's Mark' — a fate that they will die in their next battle (no save, no escape, just a certainty).
You can also transform into a crow at will, see through the eyes of any corvid within ten miles, and predict the outcome of any battle you witness (with about 80% accuracy)

objects.title: Hecatoncheires Arm
objects.text:
Greek hundred-hander. Your arm becomes a portal through which 99 phantom arms emerge — each as strong as your original, each capable of independent action. You can attack with all 100 arms simultaneously, throw 100 punches per second, or have them perform complex multi-tasking (typing, fighting, cooking, playing instruments, all at once).
The phantom arms can extend up to 30 feet and lift up to a ton each. They are slightly translucent but fully physical.

objects.title: Völund's Hand
objects.text: Norse legendary smith, the 'Weyland the Smith'. Your hand becomes a master of all crafts: any weapon, armor, tool, or device you make is of unparalleled quality (masterwork + enchantment-grade). You can forge metals that shouldn't exist, weave cloth that's stronger than kevlar, and carve wood that resists fire. you can craft a 'Named Masterpiece' — an item of true power: a sword that hungers for the blood of giants, a cloak of invisibility, boots that walk on air, anything within mythic scope. Each Named Masterpiece takes a month to complete and binds a fragment of your soul to it (if destroyed, you lose the corresponding fragment permanently)

objects.title: Hand of Kali
objects.text: Hindu goddess of destruction and liberation. You gain four additional arms (six total) and the power of destruction: anything you touch with intent to destroy crumbles. Living flesh withers, steel rusts to dust in seconds, stone erodes to sand. You can also create a 'Kali's Flame' — black fire that consumes impurities and evil, leaving the pure unharmed. you can enter 'Mahakali Form': ten arms, fifteen feet tall, able to destroy on touch anything short of divine artifacts.

objects.title: Hand of Ptah
objects.text:
Egyptian creator-god of craftsmen and architects. Your hand can shape raw matter like clay — stone, metal, wood, glass, anything you touch becomes malleable to your will. You can build a palace in a day from raw stone, craft a perfect obelisk in an hour, or repair a shattered statue with a touch.
You can also 'speak creation' — by carving a concept in hieroglyphs with your hand, you can bring that concept into being (a carved 'fire' produces flame, a carved 'life' animates a statue, a carved 'death' kills what it's carved upon). This works once per carving; carvings fade after use.

objects.title: Hand of Saint Sebastian
objects.text: A holy relic — the hand of Saint Sebastian, martyr and protector. With this hand, you can heal any wound by touch (regenerate lost limbs over hours, cure any disease, purge any poison), bless weapons to harm the supernatural, and create holy ground (a 50-meter radius around you becomes consecrated, harming undead and demons who enter); you can perform a true miracle: raise the recently dead (within 24 hours), cure any ailment no matter how supernatural, or banish any entity back to its home realm.

objects.title: Thor's Mighty Arm
objects.text: Norse — the arm that wields Mjolnir. Your arm becomes supernaturally dense and strong, capable of lifting 100 tons. You gain: the strength to throw any object with divine force (a thrown stone hits like a meteor), the ability to call Mjolnir to your hand from anywhere in the world (it flies to you, smashing through anything in its path), and the power to channel lightning through your grip. The arm is also indestructible.

objects.title: Krishna's Flute Hand
objects.text: Your hand carries the essence of Krishna's flute — able to play music that charms all living things. With a gesture (you can manifest a spectral flute), you can: calm any animal, soothe any mind (cure madness, end rage, lift depression), compel weak-willed beings to dance, or summon nature spirits to your aid. you can play 'Maharasa' — a divine melody that freezes everyone who hears it in ecstatic trance. Gods and demigods may resist; mortals cannot.

objects.title: Horus's Falcon Hand
objects.text: Egyptian — the hand of the falcon-god. Your hand takes on falcon-talons at will, capable of tearing through steel. You gain: the power to manifest spectral falcon-wings (fly at 150 mph), perfect aim with any ranged weapon (the falcon's precision), and the ability to summon a divine falcon companion that scouts for you and fights at your side.

objects.title: Lugh's Many-Skilled Hand
objects.text: Celtic — the hand of the master of all skills. Your hand gains supernatural mastery: any tool or weapon you hold is wielded at grandmaster level, any craft you attempt is executed flawlessly. You also gain the 'Sling of Lugh' — a divine sling that, when used by you, hurls stones with the force of artillery.

objects.title: [META] Auto-Mail & Alchemy (FMA)
objects.text:
Your arm is replaced with auto-mail — a high-quality prosthetic that's as functional as a real arm. More importantly, you gain the knowledge of Fullmetal Alchemist-style alchemy: by clapping your hands together (creating a transmutation circle with your body's energy), you can transmute any material into any shape, so long as you understand its composition.
You can transmute stone into weapons, metal into armor, fix broken machines, or create complex structures in seconds. With research, you can perform biological transmutation (healing, body modification), though this is dangerous.

objects.title: [META] Sage's Hand (Naruto)
objects.text:
Your hand becomes a Sage Mode focus. By channeling natural energy, you can: perform jutsu (hand-sign based ninjutsu, genjutsu, taijutsu), summon animal summons (sign a contract with any animal species at character creation), and enter Sage Mode.
You can also use Sage Art: combining natural energy with your other powers for amplified effects. The hand can mold chakra into Rasengan-style energy spheres, change their nature (fire, lightning, etc.), and even unlock a custom kekkei genkai of your choice.

objects.title: [META] Yato Arm (Gintama)
objects.text:
Your arm carries the essence of the Yato clan — an alien warrior species whose physical strength and reflexes are monstrous. With this single arm, you can punch through tank armor, catch bullets, and swing a sword fast enough to cut the air itself. Your reflexes with this arm are roughly 50× human.
The arm also has a unique 'tail-light' manifestation — when you remove your concealing bandages, your Yato power multiplies tenfold. After, you collapse.

objects.title: [META] Bonesinger's Hands
objects.text:
Your hands become Bonesinger's hands — psycho-active wraithbone-shaping tools of the Eldar Craftworlders. With a song and a gesture, you can shape raw psycho-active matter into solid form.
You gain: the ability to shape Wraithbone — a psycho-plastic material that can be as hard as steel or as flexible as cloth, that responds to your thoughts, and that can hold psychic enchantments indefinitely. You can craft Eldar-style weapons, armor, tools, and even structures from Wraithbone (a tower takes a day of singing). Wraithbone items you create are masterwork-grade and can hold three enchantments each.
You can also 'sing' existing Wraithbone to reshape it, repair it, or command it.
Requires: Spirit Stone Heart.

objects.title: [META] Gudōdama Arms (Naruto)
objects.text: Your arms can manifest Gudōdama (Truth-Seeking Orbs) — spheres of pure yin-yang release that annihilate anything they touch (no save for non-divine beings). You can shape the orbs into weapons, shields, or extensions of your body. You have nine orbs at a time; they regenerate over an hour if destroyed. You can fly by standing on the orbs, and you can nullify any non-sage-mode ninjutsu with a touch.

objects.title: [META] Stands-Arm (Jojo)
objects.text: Your arm becomes the focus for a Stand manifestation. You can summon a humanoid Stand (about your size, floating beside you) with a unique power of your design — examples: time stop, reality erasure, fire manipulation, soul extraction. The Stand fights on your behalf, is invisible to non-Stand-users, and can move up to 10 meters from you. If your Stand is destroyed, you die (and vice versa).

objects.title: [META] Zanpakutō Arm (Bleach)
objects.text: Your arm becomes the focus for a Zanpakutō — the spiritual weapon of Soul Reapers and Arrancars. The weapon manifests from your soul, taking a form that reflects your inner nature. In its sealed state, it's a katana of supernatural sharpness (cuts through steel, harms spirits, cleanses hollows). In Shikai (initial release), it gains a unique power of your design (elemental manipulation, illusion, time-alteration, etc.). In Bankai (final release), the weapon's full power manifests — typically a 10× boost with a defined domain of effect. The Zanpakutō has a spirit you can commune with in your inner world.

objects.title: [META] Nen-Arm (Hunter x Hunter)
objects.text: Your arm becomes the focus for Nen — the life-energy manipulation system of Hunter x Hunter. You gain: the ability to project and shape your aura (Ten — defense, Ren — amplification, Hatsu — unique ability). Your Hatsu develops based on your personality: Enhancement (boost your body 10×), Emission (project aura blasts), Transmutation (change aura properties — lightning, fire, etc.), Conjuration (materialize objects), Manipulation (control others), or Specialization (unique abilities). You can develop multiple Hatsu abilities with training. Nen also grants superhuman durability and perception.

objects.title: [META] Devil Bringer (Devil May Cry)
objects.text: Your right arm becomes a Devil Bringer — a demonic limb that glows with spectral blue energy. You can: extend spectral tendrils to grab and throw enemies (objects up to 10 tons), punch through supernatural defenses, and absorb the power of defeated devils (gaining a minor permanent trait from each). The arm also lets you summon the Yamato — a katana that can cut through dimensions, opening rifts between locations. You also gain superhuman reflexes (10× human) and the ability to enter Devil Trigger (demonic transformation, 5× stats) at will.

objects.title: [META] Magic Crest Arm (Fate)
objects.text: Your arm becomes the bearer of a Magic Crest from the Fate universe — a tattoo-like engraving of magical circuits that contains generations of accumulated magecraft. You gain: instant mastery of all the spells stored in the Crest (combat spells, defensive wards, healing, bounded fields), the ability to channel Od (magical energy) through your arm for amplified effects, and the power to add new spells to the Crest (any spell you learn becomes permanently stored). The Crest grows with each generation of use.

objects.title: [META] Cursed Arm (Jujutsu Kaisen)
objects.text: Your arm becomes a vessel for a Cursed Technique from Jujutsu Kaisen. You gain: a single defined Cursed Technique of your design (limitless spatial manipulation, Boogie Woogie body-swap, Mimicry, Copy, etc.), the ability to manifest Cursed Energy as physical reinforcement (10× strength, durability, speed), and a Domain Expansion — a pocket dimension where your technique is absolute (once per battle, lasts until you dismiss it or run out of Cursed Energy).

objects.title: [META] Domain Expansion Arm (JJK)
objects.text: Your arm becomes the focus for an innate domain — a pocket dimension you can manifest from Jujutsu Kaisen. You can: project your domain around you (a 30-meter sphere where your chosen rule is absolute — example rules: 'all attacks within this space are reflected back', 'time within this space loops', 'I can teleport anything within this space'). The domain is unbreakable from the outside. Inside, your power is amplified 10×.

objects.title: [META] Mystic Code Arm (Type-Moon)
objects.text: Your arm is itself a Mystic Code — a powerful magical artifact from the Type-Moon universe. You gain: a single Noble Phantasm-level ability (a defined supernatural attack or effect of your design — example: a beam that severs fate, a punch that erases existence, a grip that imprisons souls), the ability to channel unlimited magical energy through your arm, and the power to 'release' your Noble Phantasm for its true name effect (a city-leveling attack, once per day).

objects.title: [META] Stand-Arm: The World (Jojo)
objects.text: Your arm becomes the focus for The World — DIO's Stand from Jojo's Bizarre Adventure. You can: stop time for up to 10 seconds (during which only you and other time-manipulators can move), throw incredibly fast and precise punches (your Stand's fists hit hundreds of times per second), and manifest a humanoid Stand (tall, yellow, muscular) that fights on your behalf. Your Stand is invisible to non-Stand users.

objects.title: [META] Stand-Arm: King Crimson (Jojo)
objects.text: Your arm becomes the focus for King Crimson — Diavolo's Stand from Jojo. You can: 'skip' time — erase a 10-second period from existence (only you remember what happened, everyone else experiences a jump), see 10 seconds into the future at will (epitaph), and your Stand attacks with devastating power. The time-skip is disorienting to enemies who can't predict what happens during the erased time.

objects.title: [META] Conqueror's Arm (One Piece)
objects.text: Your arm becomes a master of Conqueror's Haki coating from One Piece. You can: coat your arm in black lightning-like Haki that destroys anything it touches (black blades that cut through Devil Fruit users, offensive Haki that bypasses any defense), knock out weak-willed foes with a gesture (Conqueror's Will), and project your willpower as a physical force (a wave of pressure that levels buildings).

objects.title: [META] Kamui Arm (Naruto)
objects.text: Your arm becomes the focus for Kamui — Obito's Mangekyō Sharingan ability. You can: make your arm intangible (attacks pass through it — anything you touch can be sent to your personal Kamui dimension), teleport anything you touch to the Kamui dimension (a featureless pocket space), and teleport yourself anywhere you've been before by phasing through the dimension. Your arm can also fire Kamui projectiles that send what they hit to the dimension.

objects.title: [META] Spirit Sword Arm (Yu Yu Hakusho)
objects.text: Your arm can manifest a Spirit Sword — the signature weapon of Kuwabara from Yu Yu Hakusho. You can: project your spirit energy as a sword of pure light (length controllable up to 10 feet, sharp enough to cut through dimensions), extend the sword to incredible lengths (up to a mile for short bursts), and shape your spirit energy into other weapons (whips, hammers, shields). Your spirit energy regenerates rapidly, allowing constant use.

objects.title: [META] Bankai Arm (Bleach)
objects.text: Your arm becomes permanently in a released Bankai state — the final release of a Zanpakutō from Bleach. You gain: a constant aura of spiritual pressure that crushes weak-willed foes, a unique Bankai power of your design (elemental control, time manipulation, illusion, statistical manipulation — anything within reason), and the ability to manifest your Bankai's weapon (a sword, spear, or other weapon of pure spiritual energy). Your Bankai cannot be sealed — it's always active.

rows.title: Arms
rows.titleText:
We'll save your past and self for later. Do you want to replace your left or right arm, you can also chose to do both/none. One of the downsides to replacing your arms is losing you sense of touch. Don't worry about them not fitting you or looking out of place, through my unending kindness I'll make them properly proportioned. If you've already lost some limbs I'd be willing ta regrow them for ya.

All the limbs and organs you see here are all ethically sourced, promise.

objects.title: Allfather's Leg-end
objects.text:
This one was not the easiest to get. But I'd do anything to convince you to lose your fleshy bits. Well, I guess this is pretty fleshy now that I think of it. You only get the end of it though — it would be a no-brainer to pick this one if you got all of it.
With this you can heal, cure, revive, and fix up anything you run into. The pool is generous: you can revive up to 500 people, repair an entire city, cure any disease on touch, and regenerate lost limbs in minutes. you can perform a true resurrection (someone dead up to a century, full restoration)

objects.title: Overlord's Boot
objects.text: For all the boot lickers out there! Well, unless you're one of them, be a bit weird to lick your own boot. The blue minion tribe shall fall under your command and you will get their spawning pit. Blue minions are weaker than brown minions but are capable of swimming, casting decent water and ice magic, and healing other minions. They make excellent support troops. your boot can 'ripple' the ground in a 50-meter radius, dousing everything in seawater (which extinguishes fires, reveals invisible foes, and rusts metal over time)

objects.title: Litches Leg
objects.text:
Some lich asked me to bow before him. Want a leg of pure evil? With this you'll be able to raise corpses up from the ground or, if you're feeling fancy, combine up to fifty into a monstrosity the size of a building.
All your freshly risen friends will view you as a beloved leader. wait, no, far too close to mind control. I'll just tie corpses that are most likely to be loyal to you to a custom string of fate just for you. The monstrosities will be like loyal dogs.

objects.title: Yggdrasil's Root
objects.text:
Did you know that your universe was connected to a tree? I decided to break the branch that it was connected to off — it's sort of foot-shaped? Give me a few minutes to carve it up, ok!
Wear this and nature will love ya. Plants will move out of your path and follow all your commands. You can make plants grow a hundred times their max size in the blink of an eye, awaken trees to sentience (they become loyal tree-herders), and command all mundane animals within a mile. you can manifest a 'Yggdrasil Avatar' — a 50-foot treant form with the strength to demolish a castle and the ability to call a forest to life around you.

objects.title: Mechanical One's Leg
objects.text:
Leg belonging to a currently not-yet-born god — don't worry about when it will be born. I wanted to collect their eight legs before they could get damaged. Every step you take will improve the tech around you, maxing out to tech from one thousand years into the future.
You can also interface with any technology by touch (read its code, rewrite it, learn its function instantly), and you can 'command' machines with a word — they will obey simple orders from you.

objects.title: Legy of the Lake
objects.text: They threw a sword at me! Despite its powers, it is not made of water — it's some kind of blue humanoid leg. With this you'd be able to move 500,000 gallons of water at 120 mph (enough to demolish a small building), breathe underwater, and walk on water's surface. you can summon Excalibur's tide — a tidal wave 100 feet tall that you can direct at any target within line of sight. The water recedes afterward, leaving no permanent flooding.

objects.title: Pegasus Lower Body
objects.text: Your lower body becomes that of a divine winged horse. You can fly at 150 mph with the stamina to cross continents without rest, gallop on air itself, and your hooves strike with the force of a thunderbolt (can shatter castle gates); you can enter 'Divine Steed Form' — full Pegasus form, 200 mph flight, and your neigh summons a localized thunderstorm.

objects.title: Hermes' Sandaled Legs
objects.text: Your legs become the legendary sandaled legs of Hermes himself. You can run at 600 mph on foot, walk on any surface (water, vertical walls, ceilings, clouds), and never tire from movement. Your reflexes double. you can 'Caduceus Step' — teleport to any location you can see (line of sight) or any location you've previously visited (within a thousand miles). Carrying passengers halves your range.

objects.title: Centaur Lower Body
objects.text:
Your lower body becomes that of a powerful stallion. You can gallop at 80 mph for hours, leap 40 feet from a standstill, and your hooves can shatter engine blocks. You can fight from horseback with perfect balance, fire a bow at full gallop with perfect accuracy, and trample infantry like grass.
Your stamina is effectively bottomless for physical activity; you can fight straight without fatigue.

objects.title: Spider Legs (Drider)
objects.text:
Your lower body becomes that of a giant spider — eight powerful legs, each capable of lifting a ton. You can climb any surface (including glass and ceilings), move at 40 mph, and jump 50 feet from a standstill.
You can produce silk from your spinnerets: web-lines strong enough to swing from (and restrain a rhino), web-nets, web-cocoons, web-traps. You can sense vibrations through your webs across a city block.

objects.title: Mermaid Tail
objects.text:
Your lower body becomes a powerful fish tail. Underwater, you can swim at 100 mph, breathe freely, see clearly in any water condition, and command sea-life within a mile. You can speak to the ocean itself, and it sometimes listens.
On land, your tail can shift into legs (a slow, painful transformation), but you must keep your skin wet or you'll desiccate within hours. You can manipulate water within line of sight, moving up to 100,000 gallons at a time.

objects.title: Storm Stride (Susanoo's Legs)
objects.text: Japanese storm god's legs. With each step, you can teleport up to 100 meters in any direction (appearing in a flash of lightning). You can run on storm clouds, walk on lightning bolts mid-strike, and your reflexes are 30× human. you can perform 'Thunder Step' — a 1-mile teleport that electrocutes everything within 10 meters of your starting point.

objects.title: Bone Walker Legs
objects.text:
Your legs become skeletal — pure white bone, animate and unbreakable. You can walk through any necromantic ward without triggering it, sense the dead within a mile, and raise 50 skeletons from any graveyard you walk through.
Your skeletal legs cannot be broken by any physical force, and you can detach them at will (they remain under your control, can walk on their own, and reattach on command). Each leg can be replaced with the femur of a powerful warrior to gain a fraction of their combat skills.

objects.title: Titan's Stride
objects.text:
Your legs carry the essence of a Greek Titan. You can grow to 50 feet tall at will (full Titan form), and at full size you can: kick over buildings, leap across rivers, walk through forests without slowing. Your strength scales with your size — at 50 feet, you can lift 500 tons.
Even at normal size, you have the strength of 50 men, can leap 100 feet, and your legs cannot be broken by any mundane force.

objects.title: Fenrir's Paws
objects.text: Your lower body becomes that of Fenrir, the world-devouring wolf. You can run at 200 mph, leap 200 feet, your jaws can bite through anything physical (including magical barriers), and you can grow to the size of a horse at will. you can 'Devour' — swallow any single target smaller than a building whole, trapping them in a stomach-dimension from which they cannot escape by normal means.

objects.title: Sun Wukong's Cloud Legs
objects.text:
Journey to the West. Your legs can perform the legendary Cloud-Somersault — a single leap covers 108,000 li (about 33,000 miles). You can ride clouds (summon a small cloud at will), and you cannot be restrained by any physical bond (chains, ropes, vines all slip off you).
Your legs are also supernaturally strong — a single kick can shatter a mountain, and you can grow your legs to giant size for an immense kick.

objects.title: Baba Yaga's Chicken Legs
objects.text:
Your legs become those of a giant chicken — scaly, clawed, immensely powerful. You can run at 60 mph, leap 30 feet, and your claws can disembowel a horse. More importantly, you can manifest a walking hut — a small cabin on giant chicken legs (like Baba Yaga's) that follows you around, serves as a mobile base, and can run at 40 mph on its own legs.
The hut is larger on the inside, contains a hearth that burns with magical fire (never goes out, cooks food perfectly), and has a door that opens only to those you permit.

objects.title: Hermes' Caduceus Stride
objects.text: An alternative to Hermes' Sandaled Legs — your legs intertwine with a living caduceus (the twin-snake staff). You gain: the ability to fly at 500 mph (the snakes propel you through the air), the power to heal any poison or disease with a touch of your foot, and supernatural balance (you cannot be knocked over). The caduceus-snakes also warn you of danger — they hiss when threats approach.

objects.title: Cú Chulainn's Warp-Spasm Legs
objects.text: Celtic — the legs of Ireland's greatest hero. In battle, your legs warp — twisting, elongating, becoming grotesquely muscled. You gain: the ability to enter the 'ríastrad' (warp-spasm) — a berserker state where your legs carry you at 200 mph, your leaps cover 500 feet, and your kicks can demolish castle walls. Outside of ríastrad, your legs are still supernaturally strong (10× human) and tireless.

objects.title: [META] Black Leg Style (One Piece)
objects.text:
Your legs carry the essence of Sanji's 'Black Leg' martial arts. Your kicks can shatter steel, your mobility is superhuman (you can run on air using 'Sky Walk' — kicking the air fast enough to lift yourself), and you can ignite your legs with Diable Jambe (friction-ignited fire that burns hot enough to melt steel).
After a major physical trauma (or 6 months of training), you can unlock Ifrit Jambe — an even hotter fire kick that can scorch a city block. You can also manifest a Raid Suit (a stealth armor with invisibility, flight, and enhanced defenses)

objects.title: [META] Speed Force Conduit (DC)
objects.text:
Your legs become conduits for the Speed Force — the cosmic force that powers all speedsters. You can run at any speed short of light (and at light speed, you can time-travel), phase through solid matter by vibrating your molecules, throw lightning bolts, and think at super speed.
You also gain: a 'Speed Force aura' that protects you (and anything you carry) from friction, relativistic impacts, and time-paradoxes. You can lend speed to others (making them fast for a time) or steal speed (slowing enemies to a crawl)

objects.title: [META] Geppo Legs (One Piece)
objects.text: Your legs gain the power of Geppo (Moon Step) — the Rokushiki technique that lets you kick the air itself to propel yourself. You can: fly indefinitely by kicking the air, run on air at 300 mph, leap vast distances in a single bound, and walk on any surface (including water and clouds). You also gain the other Rokushiki techniques: Soru (superhuman speed), Tekkai (iron body — turn your body to steel), Shigan (finger pistol — pierce steel with a finger), Rankyaku (kick air-blades), and Kami-e (paper art — dodge by going limp).

objects.title: [META] Soru-Soru Legs (One Piece)
objects.text: Your legs master the Rokushiki 'Soru' technique — the ability to kick the ground ten times in a blink to move at superhuman speed. You can: run at 300 mph for short bursts, dodge attacks at the speed of thought, and cross vast distances in seconds. You also gain Kami-e (paper art — become limp to dodge), and Tekkai-kenpo (combine iron-body with martial arts, allowing movement while invulnerable).

objects.title: [META] Deus Ex Machina Legs (Cyberpunk)
objects.text: Your legs become fully cybernetic — Sandevistan-grade combat implants from the Cyberpunk universe. You gain: the Sandevistan (temporarily perceive time at 1/10 speed, allowing you to perform actions seemingly instantaneously to onlookers), Mantis Blades (retractable mono-molecular blades that extend from your forearms), and reinforced hydraulic legs that let you leap 50 feet and kick through walls. The cybernetics also include a targeting AI that assists in combat.

objects.title: [META] Moonwalk Legs (Jackie Chan Adventures)
objects.text: Your legs gain supernatural agility and the Rabbit Talisman's power from Jackie Chan Adventures. You gain: superhuman speed (run at 500 mph), the ability to walk on any surface (walls, ceilings, water, clouds), supernatural jumping (leap 1,000 feet in a single bound), and the ability to perform impossible acrobatic feats (dodge bullets by weaving between them, run across falling debris, etc.). Your reflexes are 50× human.

objects.title: [META] Hyperspace Legs (Star Wars)
objects.text: Your legs can channel the Force for movement, becoming Hyperspace-grade from Star Wars. You gain: Force Speed (run at 200 mph for short bursts), Force Jump (leap 100 feet vertically), Force Leap (teleport short distances by stepping through the Force), and the ability to walk on air by stepping on the Force itself. You also gain basic Force sensitivity — telekinesis, mind tricks, precognition.

objects.title: [META] Magia Erebea Legs (Negima)
objects.text: Your legs become a vessel for Magia Erebea — the dark magic absorption technique from Negima/UQ Holder. You can: absorb any offensive magic cast at your legs and store it, then release it as a boosted counter-attack or use it to amplify your movement (boosted speed, boosted jumps, wall-running). Each absorbed spell permanently adds to your movement speed and agility.
